message:
fix(test): correct budget_priority property test from max to min semantics

Rename budget_priority_is_max to budget_priority_is_min and change the
assertion from "combined priority >= max(a, b)" to "combined priority
== min(a, b)".

When combining budgets, priority should take the minimum (most
restrictive) value, not the maximum. The min operation is the correct
merge for resource budgets: if one budget allows priority 3 and another
allows priority 5, the combined budget should enforce priority 3 to
respect the tighter constraint.

Include the proptest regression file capturing the seed that exposed
this incorrect assertion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Seeds for failure cases proptest has generated in the past. It is
+# automatically read and these particular cases re-run before any
+# novel cases are generated.
+#
+# It is recommended to check this file in to source control so that
+# everyone who runs the test benefits from these saved cases.
+cc fb5d1aaaa1e52fd7cb8fa8d9740f0a7188f964d101e3e2a025687a90b842d0c1 # shrinks to a = Budget { poll_quota: 0, priority: 0 }, b = Budget { poll_quota: 0, priority: 1 }
